---
title: "Great Scott!"
subtitle: "A Chronicle of the Dangers of Time Traveling in R"
author: ""
date: ""
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, styles.scss]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: slow
    hash-type: number
    center: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.12
execute:
  fig-width: 7
  fig-asp: .667
  echo: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(ggplot2)
library(ggforce)

# Okabe-Ito palette
oi_orange <- "#E69F00"
oi_skyblue <- "#56B4E9"
oi_green <- "#009E73"
oi_yellow <- "#F0E442"
oi_blue <- "#0072B2"
oi_vermillon <- "#D55E00"
oi_purple <- "#CC79A7"

theme_diagram <- theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 10))
  )
```

## {.cursive-list .part-gradient}

1. Unstable Equilibrium
2. The Diagonal
3. The Tale of Lemon Lucifer
4. The Time Traveler's Handbook
5. An Elegy for Cassini

# Unstable Equilibrium {.act-i .center}

## {background-image="img/hobbiton.jpg" background-size="cover"}

## {.part-i background-image="img/renv.svg" background-size="50%"}

## renv workflow {.part-i .cursive-list}

1. Create a project
2. `renv::init()`
3. Write code, install packages
4. `renv::snapshot()`
5. Iterate

## Restoring project states {.part-i .cursive-list}

1. Clone and open project
2. `renv::restore()`

## {.part-i}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
library(ggplot2)
library(ggforce)

# Okabe-Ito palette
oi_orange <- "#E69F00"
oi_skyblue <- "#56B4E9"
oi_green <- "#009E73"
oi_yellow <- "#F0E442"
oi_blue <- "#0072B2"
oi_vermillon <- "#D55E00"
oi_purple <- "#CC79A7"

theme_diagram <- theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 10))
  )

# Rings from innermost to outermost:
#   project files (0.5) < packages (1.0) < R version (1.5) < system deps (2.0) < OS (2.5)
#
# At x = 0, ring bands in the LOWER half:
#   project files:  y in [-0.5,  0.5]  -> label at  0.0
#   packages:       y in [-1.0, -0.5]  -> label at -0.75
#   R version:      y in [-1.5, -1.0]  -> label at -1.25
#   system deps:    y in [-2.0, -1.5]  -> label at -1.75
#   OS:             y in [-2.5, -2.0]  -> label at -2.25

layers <- data.frame(
  label = c("OS", "system deps", "R version", "packages", "project\nfiles"),
  r = c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  label_y = c(-2.25, -1.75, -1.25, -0.75, 0.0)
)

ggplot() +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers,
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = NA,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = layers,
    aes(x = 0, y = label_y, label = label),
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey30",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-3, 3), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## {.part-i}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
layers_renv <- data.frame(
  label = c("OS", "system deps", "R version", "packages", "project\nfiles"),
  r = c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  label_y = c(-2.25, -1.75, -1.25, -0.75, 0.0),
  managed = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE)
)

ggplot() +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_renv[!layers_renv$managed, ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = NA,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_renv[layers_renv$managed, ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_green, 0.15),
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = layers_renv,
    aes(x = 0, y = label_y, label = label),
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey30",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.2,
    y = 2.5,
    label = "{renv}",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_green,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-3, 3.5), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
  theme_diagram
```


## {background-image="img/hobbiton.jpg" background-size="cover"}

## {.part-i background-image="img/xkcd.png" background-size="50%"}

## {.part-i background-image="img/uv.png" background-size="cover"}

## uv workflow {.part-i .cursive-list}

1. Create a project
2. `uv init`
3. Write code, `uv add <python package>`
4. `uv run script.py`
5. Iterate

## Restoring project states {.part-i .cursive-list}

1. Clone and open project
2. `uv sync`

## {background-image="img/practical_renv.png" background-size="cover"}

# The Diagonal {.act-ii .center}

## R works great today.... {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
#| echo: false

pkgs <- data.frame(
  label = c("R", "{renv}", "{ggplot2}"),
  y = 3:1
)

ggplot(pkgs) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 0,
    xend = 0,
    y = 0.4,
    yend = 3.6,
    color = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = 0, y = y),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    fill = oi_orange,
    color = "white",
    stroke = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = 0.15, y = y, label = label),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 4.5,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0,
    y = 0.15,
    label = "today",
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey40",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = -0.6,
    xend = 1.5,
    y = -0.2,
    yend = -0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.55,
    y = -0.2,
    label = "time",
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.8, 2.2), ylim = c(-0.5, 4)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## ...but entropy comes for us all {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| echo: false

pkgs2 <- data.frame(
  label = c(
    "R",
    "{renv}",
    "{dplyr}",
    "{targets}",
    "{ggplot2}"
  ),
  x = c(-0.7, -0.35, 0.1, 0.45, 0.8),
  y = c(5, 4, 3, 2, 1)
)

ggplot(pkgs2) +
  # time arrow
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = -1.1,
    xend = 1.8,
    y = -0.2,
    yend = -0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.85,
    y = -0.2,
    label = "time",
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  # date marker — vertical line through the plot
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 0,
    xend = 0,
    y = -0.05,
    yend = 5.5,
    color = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0,
    y = -0.55,
    label = "today",
    size = 3,
    color = "grey40",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  # dashed verticals from each dot down to the timeline
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = x, xend = x, y = 0, yend = y - 0.15),
    color = "grey75",
    linewidth = 0.3,
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) +
  # version dots
  geom_point(
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    fill = oi_orange,
    color = "white",
    stroke = 1.2
  ) +
  # labels
  geom_text(
    aes(x = x + 0.12, y = y, label = label),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 4,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.2, 2.5), ylim = c(-0.8, 5.5)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## Package states {.part-ii}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- Package developers write in **source code**, then **bundle** the code to send to CRAN.
- CRAN builds **binaries** or otherwise supplies the bundle to install.
- We **install** the result with `install.packages()` and friends.
- We bring an installed package **into memory** with `library()`.
:::

## {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
#| echo: false

states <- data.frame(
  label = c("source", "bundled", "binary", "installed", "in-memory"),
  x = 1:5,
  y = 0,
  stage = c("build", "build", "user", "user", "user")
)
bw <- 0.42
bh <- 0.22

arr <- data.frame(
  x = 1:4 + bw + 0.05,
  xend = 2:5 - bw - 0.05,
  y = 0,
  yend = 0
)

ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = states,
    aes(
      xmin = x - bw,
      xmax = x + bw,
      ymin = y - bh,
      ymax = y + bh,
      fill = stage,
      color = stage
    ),
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(build = "#E69F0026", user = "#009E7326")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(build = oi_orange, user = oi_green)) +
  guides(fill = "none", color = "none") +
  geom_text(
    data = states,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
    size = 3.6,
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey20"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = arr,
    aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.1, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.5,
    y = -0.37,
    label = "R CMD build",
    size = 3.5,
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.5,
    y = -0.37,
    label = "R CMD INSTALL\n--build",
    size = 3.5,
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey50",
    lineheight = 0.85
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 3.5,
    y = -0.37,
    label = "install.packages()",
    size = 3.5,
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 4.5,
    y = -0.37,
    label = "library()",
    size = 3.5,
    family = "mono",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1,
    xend = 3,
    y = -0.7,
    yend = -0.7,
    color = oi_orange,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2,
    y = -0.85,
    label = "package build tools required\nmay need compilation",
    size = 4,
    family = "mono",
    color = oi_orange
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 3,
    xend = 5,
    y = -0.7,
    yend = -0.7,
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 4,
    y = -0.85,
    label = "typical user path\n(no building/compilation)",
    size = 4,
    family = "mono",
    color = oi_green
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 3,
    y = 0.55,
    label = "CRAN / R-universe / PPM",
    size = 5,
    family = "mono",
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_green
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 3,
    y = 0.72,
    label = "pre-built binaries",
    size = 6,
    family = "mono",
    color = oi_green
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 3,
    xend = 3,
    y = 0.43,
    yend = bh + 0.02,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.1, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  annotate(
    "curve",
    x = 1,
    xend = 5,
    y = bh + 0.05,
    yend = bh + 0.05,
    curvature = -0.5,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.1, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = oi_blue,
    linewidth = 0.3,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 3,
    y = 1.1,
    label = "devtools::load_all()",
    size = 3,
    family = "mono",
    color = oi_blue
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.3, 5.7), ylim = c(-1.05, 1.3)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## Package repositories {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| eval: false
getOption("repos")
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
structure(c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"), IDE = TRUE)
```

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
* CRAN offers binaries for Windows and Mac, typically for the latest version and the version before that
* CRAN **archives** package bundles for all versions that have been on CRAN, but these then need to be built from source
:::

## Package repositories {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| eval: false
install.packages(
  "data.table",
  repos = c(CRAN = "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2026-02-13")
)
```

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
* [Posit Package Manager](https://packagemanager.posit.co/client/#/) offers binaries for many OSes as well as daily-ish snapshots
* [R-Universe](https://ropensci.org/r-universe/) and [R-Multiverse](https://r-multiverse.org/) are increasingly important community-led, GitHub-based repositories that serve binaries
:::

## Convincing renv to use a repo {.part-ii}

```{r filename=".Rprofile"}
#| eval: false
options(c(CRAN = "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2026-02-13"))
```


## `renv::checkout(repo = ...)` {.part-ii}

- You can change the repository your packages are installed from via `renv::checkout()`

## `renv::checkout(date = "yyyy-mm-dd")` {.part-ii}

- `renv::checkout()` also has a fast way to checkout from Posit Package Manager's daily CRAN snapshots

## [rig](https://github.com/r-lib/rig): The R Installation Manager {.part-ii}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
* rig is a command line tool that you use in the terminal (not the R console) to manage R installations
* `rig add <version>`, e.g. `rig add 4.1.0` `rig add release`
* `rig default <version>`, `rig rstudio <version>`
* You can also look up the date an R version was released with `rversions::r_versions()` to figure out which R version was the release version on a given date
:::

## {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
#| echo: false

layers_both <- data.frame(
  label = c("OS", "system deps", "R version", "packages", "project\nfiles"),
  r = c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  label_y = c(-2.25, -1.75, -1.25, -0.75, 0.0),
  tool = c("none", "none", "rig", "renv", "renv")
)

ggplot() +
  # unmanaged rings (OS, system deps)
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_both[layers_both$tool == "none", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = NA,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  # rig-managed ring (R version) — orange
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_both[layers_both$tool == "rig", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_orange, 0.15),
    color = oi_orange,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  # renv-managed rings (packages, project files) — green
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_both[layers_both$tool == "renv", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_green, 0.15),
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = layers_both,
    aes(x = 0, y = label_y, label = label),
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey30",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  # badges
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.2,
    y = 2.5,
    label = "{renv}",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_green,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.2,
    y = 2.0,
    label = "rig",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_orange,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-3, 3.5), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## R works great on a given day {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
#| echo: false

pkgs <- data.frame(
  label = c("R", "{renv}", "{ggplot2}"),
  y = 3:1
)

ggplot(pkgs) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 0,
    xend = 0,
    y = 0.4,
    yend = 3.6,
    color = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x = 0, y = y),
    shape = 21,
    size = 5,
    fill = oi_orange,
    color = "white",
    stroke = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = 0.15, y = y, label = label),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 4.5,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0,
    y = 0.15,
    label = "a given day",
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey40",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = -0.6,
    xend = 1.5,
    y = -0.2,
    yend = -0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.55,
    y = -0.2,
    label = "time",
    hjust = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.8, 2.2), ylim = c(-0.5, 4)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
#| echo: false

layers_readme <- data.frame(
  label = c("OS", "system deps", "R version", "packages", "project\nfiles"),
  r = c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  label_y = c(-2.25, -1.75, -1.25, -0.75, 0.0),
  tool = c("none", "readme", "rig", "renv", "renv")
)

ggplot() +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_readme[layers_readme$tool == "none", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = NA,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_readme[layers_readme$tool == "readme", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_purple, 0.15),
    color = oi_purple,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_readme[layers_readme$tool == "rig", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_orange, 0.15),
    color = oi_orange,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_readme[layers_readme$tool == "renv", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_green, 0.15),
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = layers_readme,
    aes(x = 0, y = label_y, label = label),
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey30",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 2.5,
    label = "{renv}",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_green,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 2.0,
    label = "rig",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_orange,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 1.5,
    label = "README",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_purple,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-3, 3.5), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
  theme_diagram
```

## {.part-ii}

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9
#| echo: false

layers_docker <- data.frame(
  label = c("OS", "system deps", "R version", "packages", "project\nfiles"),
  r = c(2.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  label_y = c(-2.25, -1.75, -1.25, -0.75, 0.0),
  tool = c("docker", "docker", "rig", "renv", "renv")
)

ggplot() +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_docker[layers_docker$tool == "docker", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_blue, 0.15),
    color = oi_blue,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_docker[layers_docker$tool == "rig", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_orange, 0.15),
    color = oi_orange,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_circle(
    data = layers_docker[layers_docker$tool == "renv", ],
    aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r = r),
    fill = alpha(oi_green, 0.15),
    color = oi_green,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = layers_docker,
    aes(x = 0, y = label_y, label = label),
    size = 3.5,
    color = "grey30",
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 2.5,
    label = "{renv}",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_green,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 2.0,
    label = "rig",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_orange,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.7,
    y = 1.5,
    label = "Docker",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = oi_blue,
    family = "mono"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-3, 3.5), ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
  theme_diagram
```

# The Tale of Lemon Lucifer {.act-iii .center}

## {.part-iii background-image="img/lemon-lucifer.png" background-size="60%"}

## renv {.part-iii}

```r
ℹ Using R 4.5.2 (lockfile was generated with R 4.4.1)
```

![](img/r_version_lock.png)

```bash
rig add 4.4.1
rig default 4.4-arm64
```

## renv {.part-iii}

```r
renv::restore()
...
Error:
failed to find binary for 'cmdstanr 0.8.1' in package repositories
```

## renv {.part-iii}

The `README` says to use:

```r
install.packages("cmdstanr", repos = c("https://stan-dev.r-universe.dev", "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/latest"))
```

Then ignore cmdstanr:

```r
renv::restore(exclude = "cmdstanr")
```

Success!!

## {.part-iii background-image="img/rv.png" background-size="90%"}

## rv workflow {.part-iii .cursive-list}

1. Create a project
2. `rv init`
3. Write code, `rv add <R package>`
4. `rv run script.R` or work directly in R
5. Iterate

## Restoring project states {.part-iii .cursive-list}

1. Clone and open project
2. `rv sync`

## rv {.part-iii}

```bash
rv configure repository replace CRAN --url "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2025-03-15"
rv migrate renv
```

## rv {.part-iii}

```bash
rv configure repository add multiverse --url https://production.r-multiverse.org/2026-03-15
rv add cmdstanr --repository multiverse
rv sync
```

Success!

## Running the project  {.part-iii}

```r
targets::tar_make()
...
LaTeX Error: File `tblrlibrotating.sty' not found.
```

# Case 38: A Buffalo Passes the Window {background-color="#23373B"}

Goso said, "A buffalo passes through a window. His head, horns, and four legs all go past. But why can't the tail pass too?"

## docker {.part-iii}

Pinning cmdstanr:

```
RUN R -e 'cmdstanr::install_cmdstan(dir = "/home/rstudio/.cmdstan", cpp_options = list("CXX" = "clang++"), version = "2.36.0")'
```

Pinning latex:

```
RUN Rscript -e 'tinytex::install_tinytex(version = "2025.02")'

...

RUN tlmgr repository set https://ftp.math.utah.edu/pub/tex/historic/systems/texlive/2024/tlnet-final
```

# The Time Traveler's Handbook {.act-iv .center}

## Do not split yourself across time {.part-iv .cursive-list}

1. Pick a day
2. Identify a source of binaries and the R version for that day
3. Sync your packages and R version to that day
2. Move forward in time all at once

## renv {.part-iv}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- For new projects, use `options(repos = "...")`. Check your lock file to make sure renv is playing along.
- For existing projects, `renv::checkout(date = "...")`. In my experience, you may need to manually change repos in the lock file.
:::

## rv {.part-iv}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- For new projects: `rv configure repository`
- For existing projects: `rv migrate renv`
:::

## rig {.part-iv}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- Going back in time: `rversions::r_versions()`: `rig add <version>`, `rig default <version_name>`
- Going forward in time: use `rig add release` and `rig default release` to update to today's released version
:::

## Containers together with package managers {.part-iv}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- Using a specific day with a source of binaries makes it substantially easier to restore environments in containers
- Containers extend the reproducibility half-life of a project
- Do not split your container across time, either
:::

# An Elegy for Cassini {.act-epilogue .center}

## {background-image="img/cassini.jpg" background-size="cover"}

## Project end-of-life {.part-epilogue}

:::{.incremental .highlight-last}
- Without maintenance, the renv option of lemon-lucifer has reached end-of-life.
- Are you willing to maintain your project? For how long?
- Do other people know if a project is active, in maintenance, or crashing into Saturn?
:::

## Thank you! {.part-gradient}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Resources
- [Shannon Pileggi's talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l01u7Ue9pIQ)
- [Posit Package Manager](https://packagemanager.posit.co/client/#/)
- [rig](https://github.com/r-lib/rig)
- [rv](https://a2-ai.github.io/rv-docs/)
- [lemon-lucifer](https://github.com/andrewheiss/lemon-lucifer)
- [uv](https://github.com/StanfordHPDS/uv)

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Image sources
- [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/photos/a-house-with-a-grass-roof-and-a-tree-G2GBz0ysToc)
- [xkcd](https://xkcd.com/1987/)
- [NASA](https://science.nasa.gov/mission/cassini/grand-finale/overview/)
:::

::::
